title: silene velebitica
intro: Silene velebitica, also known as Velebitian silene, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Balkan Peninsula. This perennial herb is a member of the carnation family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. Silene velebitica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various forms of supplementation.
